axes_surfaced.name: phase-decomposition-granularity

options_considered.label: A
options_considered.shape: 1 monolithic phase (port + verify + dispatch in one swing)
options_considered.rejection_rationale: no falsifier checkpoints; failure mode is binary (works or doesn't); violates seed's design-then-impl gradient

options_considered.label: B
options_considered.rejection_rationale: elides Phase 0 collision investigation; bakes in untested HEXA_RC↔json-line collision risk

options_considered.label: C
options_considered.shape: 4 phases (Phase 0 investigate / Phase 1 port Text / Phase 2 byte-trace harness / Phase 3 remote dispatch loop)
options_considered.rejection_rationale: (SELECTED) maps each falsifier F1..F7 to a specific phase; honors single-primitive non-goal; phase exit criteria are deterministic

options_considered.label: D
options_considered.shape: 6+ micro-phases (one per primitive method, per invariant, per env step)
options_considered.rejection_rationale: premature decomposition before first impl observation; over-fits to a phantom risk model

axes_surfaced.rationale: minimum phase count where every pre-registered falsifier (F1..F7) maps cleanly to one phase's exit criterion; aligns with beta_qrng pick (89 mod 7 = 5, clamped to 4)

axes_surfaced.name: handoff-channel-pick

options_considered.label: A
options_considered.shape: json-line-stdout — hexa writes one JSON line per frame; ts reads via line buffer
options_considered.rejection_rationale: (SELECTED) lowest surface; fits HEXA_RC sentinel idiom; collision risk is investigatable in Phase 0

options_considered.label: B
options_considered.shape: fd3-binary-frame — hexa writes packed binary on FD 3
options_considered.rejection_rationale: needs hexa-side FD-3 support not confirmed in current runtime; deferred to second impl drill if json-line measurably underperforms

options_considered.label: C
options_considered.rejection_rationale: OS-level coupling + crash-recovery complexity disproportionate to first-port (single Text primitive) workload

options_considered.label: D
options_considered.rejection_rationale: leaves Phase 0 with no concrete artifact to investigate; falsifier F2 cannot be pre-registered without a concrete channel pick

axes_surfaced.decision: A — json-line-stdout with HEXA_RC collision investigation as Phase 0 gate
axes_surfaced.rationale: predecessor 05:47Z drill already preferred A; making the collision risk a phase-zero deliverable (rather than a deferred risk) resolves the open question before any frame bytes are emitted

axes_surfaced.name: byte-trace-verification-locus

options_considered.label: A
options_considered.shape: ts-side standalone checker (separate node script consuming recorded byte traces)
options_considered.rejection_rationale: (SELECTED) ts side already owns escape-emit boundary in pi-tui; harness lives where the bytes are produced; impl-agnostic to whether hexa or ts wrote the cells

options_considered.label: B
options_considered.shape: hexa-side checker (hexa parses its own emitted bytes)
options_considered.rejection_rationale: violates ownership invariant — hexa never writes terminal escapes per bridge contract, so it has no authoritative escape-syntax model

options_considered.label: C
options_considered.rejection_rationale: violates raw_8 hexa-only — no .py emit allowed; .ts is permitted (pi-tui surface) but .py is not

options_considered.label: D
options_considered.rejection_rationale: byte traces contain non-text escape sequences; bash/grep parsing fragile; recipe pass/fail signature requires structural assertions (CUP-position equality, wrapper presence) better expressed in code

axes_surfaced.rationale: byte traces produced by pi-tui ↔ harness lives in ts; mirrors the ownership invariant from predecessor bridge_contract.ownership_invariants[2] ('the diff kernel never reaches into hexa state; it diffs CellGrid_t against CellGrid_{t+1} only')

axes_surfaced.name: remote-dispatch-shape

options_considered.label: A
options_considered.shape: ssh + tar+exec (drill-stratum standard pattern from earlier cycles)
options_considered.rejection_rationale: works but duplicates orchestration logic that bench orchestrator already implements

options_considered.label: B
options_considered.shape: bench orchestrator dispatch (existing infra routes to {ubu1,ubu2,herz})
options_considered.rejection_rationale: (SELECTED) reuses proven routing; herz primary per qrng tie-break (byte 89 mod 3 = 2); no new infra falsifiers needed

options_considered.label: C
options_considered.shape: dedicated tui-bridge binary on remote (purpose-built launcher)
options_considered.rejection_rationale: premature optimization — first impl drill ships one Text primitive; dedicated binary is appropriate after second port (Box) at earliest

options_considered.label: D
options_considered.rejection_rationale: no k8s infrastructure in scope for current drill workloads; would require a parallel cycle to introduce

axes_surfaced.rationale: bench orchestrator already routes drill workloads to {ubu1, ubu2, herz}; reusing it satisfies F3 (no HEXA_LOCAL/NO_CAP/HEXA_ALLOW_LOCAL_FALLBACK) without inventing new routing surface

implementation_plan.scope: phased plan to bridge hexa-lang to pi-tui through one primitive (Text) end-to-end on remote compute, with byte-trace falsifier verification of the I1-I5 render contract

phases.name: investigate-and-pin

deliverables.name: HEXA_RC stdout sentinel ↔ json-line-stdout collision investigation
deliverables.exit_criterion: assert state/investigations/hexa_rc_jsonline_collision.md exists AND records yes/no verdict + recommended delimiter (e.g. HEXA_FRAME=<n> sentinel or JSON-RPC newline-delimited frames) if collision is real

deliverables.name: remote {ubu1,ubu2,herz} reachability + HEXA_LOCAL/NO_CAP env unset confirmation
deliverables.exit_criterion: assert state/investigations/remote_dispatch_preflight.md exists AND records ssh + env-grep results for all three hosts

deliverables.name: Text primitive surface enumeration (props, methods, render output shape) read from /Users/ghost/core/hive/packages/tui/src/components/text.ts
deliverables.exit_criterion: assert state/investigations/text_primitive_surface.md exists AND lists every public symbol exported by text.ts with its signature

non_goals: DO NOT emit json-line bytes — investigation only
phases.dispatch: local design read-only (no compute workload — investigation cycle reads files only)
phases.exit_criterion_summary: assert all three deliverables produced as state/investigations/*.md artifacts; assert collision verdict resolved before Phase 1 entry

phases.name: port-text-primitive

deliverables.name: core/tui/text.hexa — bounded mirror of pi-tui Text component (props + render(state,size)→CellGrid)
deliverables.exit_criterion: assert /Users/ghost/core/hive/core/tui/text.hexa exists AND wc -l ≤ 250 (bounded; mirror discipline from keybindings.hexa = 505 LOC for a much larger component)

deliverables.name: core/tui/cell_grid.hexa — CellGrid type (sum-of-products: cell={x,y,glyph,fg,bg,attrs}) + serialize_to_json_line
deliverables.exit_criterion: assert /Users/ghost/core/hive/core/tui/cell_grid.hexa exists AND defines CellGrid + serialize function

deliverables.name: core/tui/bridge_main.hexa — entry point: emit one frame as json-line on stdout, then HEXA_RC=0
deliverables.exit_criterion: assert /Users/ghost/core/hive/core/tui/bridge_main.hexa exists AND ends with HEXA_RC=0 sentinel emit

non_goals: DO NOT port Box or any other primitive
DO NOT implement event channel (stdin) yet — frame-emit only
DO NOT write terminal escapes from hexa side (ownership invariant)
phases.dispatch: remote ONLY via bench orchestrator → herz primary (per beta_qrng_byte=89 mod 3 = 2), ubu1 secondary, ubu2 tertiary; HEXA_LOCAL/HEXA_ALLOW_LOCAL_FALLBACK/NO_CAP FORBIDDEN per F3
phases.exit_criterion_summary: assert hexac compile of core/tui/bridge_main.hexa on remote returns 0; assert one stdout JSON line is recorded with shape {v:1, cells:[...]} during smoke run

phases.name: byte-trace-harness-i1-i5

deliverables.name: ts-side checker packages/tui/test/byte_trace_check.ts (or sibling location TBD by impl cycle)
deliverables.exit_criterion: assert checker exists AND imports no hexa-side state (impl-agnostic byte-trace consumer)

phases.fixture_recipe_source: predecessor 05:47Z drill witness falsifier_recipe_table.recipes[] (lifted verbatim — fixture names + harness sketches + pass/fail byte signatures)
non_goals: DO NOT modify the I1-I5 invariants themselves
DO NOT add new invariants in this phase — those belong to a separate render-contract-extension cycle
DO NOT emit fixture data from hexa side; fixture files are static JSON authored once
